content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Addressed all three pending amendments for planned-elsewhere validation correctness. Amendment 1: Added 5 unit tests for extract_pe_bead_ids in task_prompt_contract.rs covering normal bullet format, no PE section, PE section with no bullets, multiple beads, and stops at next heading. Amendments 2+3 (same root cause — validation timing): Moved PE validation from the post-commit recorder (record_planned_elsewhere_amendments in engine.rs) to BEFORE the restart decision in execute_final_review_panel (final_review.rs). After building final_accepted_amendments, the function now calls extract_pe_bead_ids on the project_prompt (already in scope) and strips mapped_to_bead_id (sets to None) for any amendment whose value is not in the allowed set. Fail-closed: when the allowed set is empty (no PE section in prompt, or prose-only 'no explicit planned-elsewhere items' text), ALL mapped_to_bead_id values are stripped, preventing hallucinated bead IDs from suppressing restarts. The post-commit recorder in engine.rs was also updated to fail closed (removed the !allowed_pe_ids.is_empty() guard) for defense-in-depth. Since the aggregate now only contains validated PE mappings, reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings in success_reconciliation.rs inherently operates on already-validated data.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Amendment 1: Added 5 unit tests for extract_pe_bead_ids in task_prompt_contract.rs — extract_pe_bead_ids_normal_bullets (two bullet items parsed), extract_pe_bead_ids_no_pe_section_returns_empty, extract_pe_bead_ids_pe_section_no_bullets_returns_empty (prose text with no bullets), extract_pe_bead_ids_multiple_beads_captured (three beads all captured), extract_pe_bead_ids_stops_at_next_heading (parsing stops at ## Review Policy heading, bead-Y not captured).
2. [Completed] Amendments 2+3: In execute_final_review_panel (final_review.rs), after building final_accepted_amendments and before both the force-complete check and the restart decision, added PE validation: extract allowed PE set from project_prompt via task_prompt_contract::extract_pe_bead_ids, iterate mutable amendments and set mapped_to_bead_id to None when the value is not in the allowed set OR when the allowed set is empty (fail-closed). This ensures invalid PE classifications are stripped BEFORE the FinalReviewAggregatePayload is built and the restart_required decision is made.
3. [Completed] Amendments 2+3: In record_planned_elsewhere_amendments (engine.rs), changed the validation guard from !allowed_pe_ids.is_empty() && !allowed_pe_ids.contains(mapped_to) to !allowed_pe_ids.contains(mapped_to), removing the skip-when-empty escape hatch. This provides defense-in-depth fail-closed validation in the post-commit recorder, consistent with the upstream validation in final_review.rs.
4. [Completed] Ran cargo test (758 passed, 0 failed), cargo clippy (no new warnings from changed files), cargo fmt --check (clean).

## Validation Evidence

- cargo test: 758 passed, 0 failed across all test suites (lib, integration, unit, doc-tests)
- cargo test extract_pe_bead: 5 new tests pass (normal_bullets, no_pe_section_returns_empty, pe_section_no_bullets_returns_empty, multiple_beads_captured, stops_at_next_heading)
- cargo fmt --check: exit 0, no formatting issues
- cargo clippy: no new warnings from any changed file (task_prompt_contract.rs, final_review.rs, engine.rs); all 19 warnings are pre-existing in unrelated files
- Amendment 1 verified: 5 tests cover all requested scenarios — normal bullet format, no PE section, PE section without bullets, multiple beads, and stops at next heading boundary
- Amendments 2+3 verified: PE validation in final_review.rs occurs at line 1516-1525 (after final_accepted_amendments construction at line 1506, before force-complete check at line 1530 and restart decision at line 1581). The fail-closed behavior is confirmed by extract_pe_bead_ids_pe_section_no_bullets_returns_empty test — the 'No explicit planned-elsewhere items' prose text returns an empty set, which causes the validation loop to strip all mapped_to_bead_id values. Defense-in-depth in engine.rs at line 7641 also rejects when allowed set is empty.
- Amendments 2+3 verified: reconstruct_missing_pe_mappings in success_reconciliation.rs inherently operates on validated aggregates since the upstream validation strips invalid mapped_to_bead_id values before the aggregate is built and committed — no changes needed there.
- git diff --stat HEAD -- src/: 3 files changed, 63 insertions, 6 deletions (task_prompt_contract.rs +43, engine.rs +4/-6, final_review.rs +16/-0)
- Files modified: src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs (5 new tests), src/contexts/workflow_composition/final_review.rs (PE validation before restart decision), src/contexts/workflow_composition/engine.rs (fail-closed validation in post-commit recorder)

## Outstanding Risks

- Pre-existing clippy warnings (19 from code outside this change) cause cargo clippy -- -D warnings to fail; none are introduced by this change.
- The fail-closed behavior means that if the prompt file cannot be read in execute_final_review_panel, the function returns AppError::CorruptRecord (line 274) before reaching the PE validation code. This is correct — the prompt is required for the review panel to function at all.
- If a future prompt format change causes extract_pe_bead_ids to fail to parse valid PE bead IDs, the empty set will cause all mapped_to_bead_id values to be stripped (fail-closed). This is the desired conservative behavior — false negatives (stripping valid PE mappings) trigger a restart, while false positives (accepting invalid PE mappings) would silently drop findings.
